---
title: "MALE Adrenal Pubertal Age"
author: ""
date: ""
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
#Loading Data
setwd("")
abcd <- readRDS("")
variable.names(abcd)

library(magrittr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(caret)
library(mgcv) # for GAM
library(randomForest)
library(xgboost)
library(fmsb) # for radar chart
library(mice) # for imputation (if needed)

#Remove participants sub-53CVF7NB sub-K2KR1DUA
abcd <- abcd %>%
  filter(participant_id != "sub-53CVF7NB")

abcd <- abcd %>%
  filter(participant_id != "sub-K2KR1DUA")

```

```{r Initial Male Prediction of Age}

#filter for males only

abcd_male <- abcd %>%
  filter(ab_g_stc__cohort_sex==1)



#remove 999 from puberty dataset
abcd_male[abcd_male == 999] <- NA
abcd_male[abcd_male == 777] <- NA


summary(abcd_male)


```

```{r Predicting Age - leakage-safe pipeline}

# -----------------------------
# 1. Define outcome + predictors
# -----------------------------
target_str <- "ph_y_pds_age"

predictors_age_YESpuberty <- c(
  "ph_p_pds_002",#
  "ph_p_pds_003",#
  "ph_y_pds_002",#
  "ph_y_pds_003",#
  "ph_y_phs__dhea_mean"#
  )

# Build formula
formula_obj <- as.formula(
  paste(target_str, "~", paste(predictors_age_YESpuberty, collapse = " + "))
)
print(formula_obj)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Create analysis dataset
#    Keep only needed columns
# -----------------------------
analysis_df <- abcd_male %>%
  select(participant_id,all_of(c(target_str, predictors_age_YESpuberty))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(.data[[target_str]]))

# Check outcome missingness after filtering
sum(is.na(analysis_df[[target_str]]))

# -----------------------------
# 3. Train/test split FIRST
# -----------------------------
set.seed(123)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(
  analysis_df[[target_str]],
  p = 0.8,
  list = FALSE
)

train_raw <- analysis_df[trainIndex, ]
test_raw  <- analysis_df[-trainIndex, ]

train_ids <- train_raw$participant_id
test_ids <- test_raw$participant_id

train_raw <- train_raw %>% select(-participant_id)
test_raw <- test_raw %>% select(-participant_id)

cat("Training N =", nrow(train_raw), "\n")
cat("Test N =", nrow(test_raw), "\n")

# -----------------------------
# 4. Run MICE on TRAINING DATA ONLY
# -----------------------------
library(mice)

impute_train <- mice(
  train_raw,
  m = 5,
  maxit = 10,
  seed = 123,
  printFlag = TRUE
)

train_df <- complete(impute_train, action = "all")

# -----------------------------
# 5. Impute TEST DATA SEPARATELY
#    Outcome is retained but not used
#    to define train imputation
# -----------------------------
# Initialize mice on test data
init_test <- mice(
  test_raw,
  m = 5,
  maxit = 0,
  printFlag = FALSE
)

meth_test <- init_test$method
pred_test <- init_test$predictorMatrix

# Do NOT impute outcome
meth_test[target_str] <- ""

# Do NOT use outcome to impute predictors in test set
pred_test[, target_str] <- 0

impute_test <- mice(
  test_raw,
  m = 5,
  method = meth_test,
  predictorMatrix = pred_test,
  maxit = 10,
  seed = 456,
  printFlag = TRUE
)

test_df <- complete(impute_test, action = "all")

# -----------------------------
# 6. Scale predictors using TRAINING ONLY
# -----------------------------
numeric_predictors <- predictors_age_YESpuberty[
  sapply(train_df[[1]][, predictors_age_YESpuberty, drop = FALSE], is.numeric)
]

# Estimate scaling parameters from first imputed training set
# (keeps workflow simple and aligned across imputations)
pp <- preProcess(
  train_df[[1]][, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE],
  method = c("center", "scale")
)

# Apply scaling to each imputed training dataset
train_df <- lapply(train_df, function(df) {
  df[, numeric_predictors] <- predict(pp, df[, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE])
  df
})

# Apply SAME scaling parameters to each imputed test dataset
test_df <- lapply(test_df, function(df) {
  df[, numeric_predictors] <- predict(pp, df[, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE])
  df
})

# Confirm scaling on training predictors
summary(train_df[[1]][, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE])
```

```{r fit ML models}
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)

# Step 2 â€” Fit models

set.seed(123)

# -------------------------------
# 1. Linear Regression (LM)
# -------------------------------
lm_fit <- lapply(seq_along(train_df), function(i) {
  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("Fitting LM â€” Imputation", i, "\n")
  cat("==============================\n")
  
  train(
    formula_obj,
    data = train_df[[i]],
    method = "lm",
    trControl = ctrl
  )
})

# -------------------------------
# 2. Generalized Additive Model (GAM)
# -------------------------------
gam_fit <- lapply(seq_along(train_df), function(i) {
  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("Fitting GAM â€” Imputation", i, "\n")
  cat("==============================\n")
  
  train(
    formula_obj,
    data = train_df[[i]],
    method = "gam",
    trControl = ctrl
  )
})

# -------------------------------
# 3. Random Forest (RF)
# -------------------------------
rf_fit <- lapply(seq_along(train_df), function(i) {
  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("Fitting Random Forest â€” Imputation", i, "\n")
  cat("==============================\n")
  
  randomForest(
    formula = formula_obj,
    data = train_df[[i]],
    ntree = 500,
    importance = TRUE,
    do.trace = 50  # prints progress every 50 trees
  )
})

# -------------------------------
# 4. XGBoost (XGB)
# -------------------------------
# -------------------------------
# 4. XGBoost (XGB)
# -------------------------------
xgb_ctrl <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 5,
  verboseIter = TRUE,
  allowParallel = TRUE
)

# Define a tuneGrid with tree parameters
xgb_tuneGrid <- expand.grid(
  nrounds = 100, # Number of boosting rounds
  max_depth = 6, # Maximum depth of trees
  eta = 0.3,     # Learning rate
  gamma = 0,     # Minimum loss reduction
  colsample_bytree = 0.8, # Subsample ratio of columns for each tree
  min_child_weight = 1,    # Minimum number of data points in a child
  subsample = 1
)

xgb_fit <- lapply(seq_along(train_df), function(i) {
  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("Fitting XGBoost â€” Imputation", i, "\n")
  cat("==============================\n")
  
  train(
    formula_obj,
    data = train_df[[i]],
    method = "xgbTree",
    trControl = xgb_ctrl,
    tuneGrid = xgb_tuneGrid,
    verbose = 1  # This may help with additional logging during training
  )
})

# -------------------------------
# Predictions on test sets
# -------------------------------
predict_avg <- function(fit_list, test_list, method_type = c("caret","rf")) {
  method_type <- match.arg(method_type)
  
  preds <- sapply(seq_along(fit_list), function(i) {
    if (method_type == "caret") {
      predict(fit_list[[i]], newdata = test_list[[i]])
    } else if (method_type == "rf") {
      predict(fit_list[[i]], newdata = test_list[[i]])
    }
  })
  
  rowMeans(preds)  # average across imputations
}

lm_preds_avg  <- predict_avg(lm_fit, test_df, method_type = "caret")
gam_preds_avg <- predict_avg(gam_fit, test_df, method_type = "caret")
rf_preds_avg  <- predict_avg(rf_fit, test_df, method_type = "rf")
xgb_preds_avg <- predict_avg(xgb_fit, test_df, method_type = "caret")
```
## Including Plots

```{r ML model interpretation}

# -------------------------------
# Compute performance metrics
# -------------------------------
calc_metrics <- function(preds, actual) {
  c(
    RMSE = RMSE(preds, actual),
    MAE  = MAE(preds, actual),
    R2   = R2(preds, actual)
  )
}

results <- data.frame(
  Method = c("LM", "GAM", "Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
  rbind(
    calc_metrics(lm_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]]),
    calc_metrics(gam_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]]),
    calc_metrics(rf_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]]),
    calc_metrics(xgb_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]])
  )
)

print(results)

library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(randomForest)
library(xgboost)

# -------------------------------
# Helper function: caret models (LM, GAM)
# -------------------------------
get_caret_varimp <- function(fit_obj) {
  # fit_obj can be a single caret model or a list of caret models (e.g., per imputation)
  fits_list <- if (inherits(fit_obj, "train")) list(fit_obj) else fit_obj

  imp_list <- lapply(fits_list, function(one_fit) {
    vi <- caret::varImp(one_fit)$importance

    # vi sometimes is a vector-like 1-col df; sometimes multiple cols
    vi_df <- data.frame(
      Feature = rownames(vi),
      Importance = as.numeric(vi[[1]]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    rownames(vi_df) <- NULL
    vi_df
  })

  # align by Feature, fill missing with 0, then average
  all_features <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(imp_list, `[[`, "Feature"))))

  imp_mat <- sapply(imp_list, function(df_imp) {
    vals <- setNames(df_imp$Importance, df_imp$Feature)
    out <- vals[all_features]
    out[is.na(out)] <- 0
    as.numeric(out)
  })

  avg_imp <- rowMeans(imp_mat)

  out_df <- data.frame(Feature = all_features, Importance = avg_imp, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  out_df <- out_df[order(out_df$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  out_df
}

# -------------------------------
# Helper function: Random Forest
# -------------------------------
get_rf_varimp <- function(rf_list) {
  imp_list <- lapply(rf_list, function(m) {
    # Use %IncMSE for importance
    data.frame(Feature = rownames(m$importance),
               Importance = m$importance[, "%IncMSE"])
  })
  
  # Average importance across imputations
  all_features <- unique(unlist(lapply(imp_list, function(x) x$Feature)))
  
  avg_imp <- sapply(all_features, function(f) {
    mean(sapply(imp_list, function(x) {
      if (f %in% x$Feature) x$Importance[x$Feature == f] else NA
    }), na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  
  data.frame(Feature = all_features, Importance = avg_imp) %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
}

# -------------------------------
# Helper function: XGBoost
# -------------------------------
get_xgb_varimp <- function(xgb_list) {
  imp_list <- lapply(xgb_list, function(m) {
    imp <- xgb.importance(model = m$finalModel)
    data.frame(Feature = imp$Feature, Importance = imp$Gain)
  })
  
  # Average importance across imputations
  all_features <- unique(unlist(lapply(imp_list, function(x) x$Feature)))
  
  avg_imp <- sapply(all_features, function(f) {
    mean(sapply(imp_list, function(x) {
      if (f %in% x$Feature) x$Importance[x$Feature == f] else NA
    }), na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  
  data.frame(Feature = all_features, Importance = avg_imp) %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
}

# -------------------------------
# Generate importance data frames
# -------------------------------
lm_imp  <- get_caret_varimp(lm_fit)
gam_imp <- get_caret_varimp(gam_fit)
rf_imp  <- get_rf_varimp(rf_fit)
xgb_imp <- get_xgb_varimp(xgb_fit)

# -------------------------------
# Plot function
# -------------------------------
plot_varimp <- function(df, title) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = title, x = "Feature", y = "Importance") +
    theme_minimal()
}

# -------------------------------
# Plot all models
# -------------------------------
plot_varimp(lm_imp, "LM Variable Importance")
plot_varimp(gam_imp, "GAM Variable Importance")
plot_varimp(rf_imp, "Random Forest Variable Importance")
plot_varimp(xgb_imp, "XGBoost Variable Importance")

# Combine the feature importance data into a single data frame
combine_varimp <- function(lm_imp, gam_imp, rf_imp, xgb_imp) {
  # Add model names to each importance data frame
  lm_imp$Model <- "Linear Regression"
  gam_imp$Model <- "Generalized Additive Model"
  rf_imp$Model <- "Random Forest"
  xgb_imp$Model <- "XGBoost"
  
  # Combine all model importance data frames
  combined_imp <- rbind(lm_imp, gam_imp, rf_imp, xgb_imp)
  
  # Make sure Features are factor to handle the plot properly
  combined_imp$Feature <- factor(combined_imp$Feature, levels = unique(combined_imp$Feature))
  
  return(combined_imp)
}

# Combine importance from all models
combined_imp <- combine_varimp(lm_imp, gam_imp, rf_imp, xgb_imp)

# Print the combined feature importance data
print(combined_imp)
```

```{r ML model interpretation 1a}

# -------------------------------
# Compute performance metrics
# -------------------------------
calc_metrics <- function(preds, actual) {
  c(
    RMSE = RMSE(preds, actual),
    MAE  = MAE(preds, actual),
    R2   = R2(preds, actual)
  )
}

results <- data.frame(
  Method = c("LM", "GAM", "Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
  rbind(
    calc_metrics(lm_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]]),
    calc_metrics(gam_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]]),
    calc_metrics(rf_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]]),
    calc_metrics(xgb_preds_avg, test_df[[1]][[target_str]])
  )
)

print(results)

library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(randomForest)
library(xgboost)

# -------------------------------
# Helper function: caret models (LM, GAM)
# -------------------------------
get_caret_varimp <- function(fit_obj) {
  # fit_obj can be a single caret model or a list of caret models (e.g., per imputation)
  fits_list <- if (inherits(fit_obj, "train")) list(fit_obj) else fit_obj

  imp_list <- lapply(fits_list, function(one_fit) {
    vi <- caret::varImp(one_fit)$importance

    # vi sometimes is a vector-like 1-col df; sometimes multiple cols
    vi_df <- data.frame(
      Feature = rownames(vi),
      Importance = as.numeric(vi[[1]]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    rownames(vi_df) <- NULL
    vi_df
  })

  # align by Feature, fill missing with 0, then average
  all_features <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(imp_list, `[[`, "Feature"))))

  imp_mat <- sapply(imp_list, function(df_imp) {
    vals <- setNames(df_imp$Importance, df_imp$Feature)
    out <- vals[all_features]
    out[is.na(out)] <- 0
    as.numeric(out)
  })

  avg_imp <- rowMeans(imp_mat)

  out_df <- data.frame(Feature = all_features, Importance = avg_imp, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  out_df <- out_df[order(out_df$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  out_df
}

# -------------------------------
# Helper function: Random Forest
# -------------------------------
get_rf_varimp <- function(rf_list) {
  imp_list <- lapply(rf_list, function(m) {
    # Use %IncMSE for importance
    data.frame(Feature = rownames(m$importance),
               Importance = m$importance[, "%IncMSE"])
  })
  
  # Average importance across imputations
  all_features <- unique(unlist(lapply(imp_list, function(x) x$Feature)))
  
  avg_imp <- sapply(all_features, function(f) {
    mean(sapply(imp_list, function(x) {
      if (f %in% x$Feature) x$Importance[x$Feature == f] else NA
    }), na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  
  data.frame(Feature = all_features, Importance = avg_imp) %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
}

# -------------------------------
# Helper function: XGBoost
# -------------------------------
get_xgb_varimp <- function(xgb_list) {
  imp_list <- lapply(xgb_list, function(m) {
    imp <- xgb.importance(model = m$finalModel)
    data.frame(Feature = imp$Feature, Importance = imp$Gain)
  })
  
  # Average importance across imputations
  all_features <- unique(unlist(lapply(imp_list, function(x) x$Feature)))
  
  avg_imp <- sapply(all_features, function(f) {
    mean(sapply(imp_list, function(x) {
      if (f %in% x$Feature) x$Importance[x$Feature == f] else NA
    }), na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  
  data.frame(Feature = all_features, Importance = avg_imp) %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
}

# -------------------------------
# Generate importance data frames
# -------------------------------
lm_imp  <- get_caret_varimp(lm_fit)
gam_imp <- get_caret_varimp(gam_fit)
rf_imp  <- get_rf_varimp(rf_fit)
xgb_imp <- get_xgb_varimp(xgb_fit)

# -------------------------------
# Plot function
# -------------------------------
plot_varimp <- function(df, title) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = title, x = "Feature", y = "Importance") +
    theme_minimal()
}

# -------------------------------
# Plot all models
# -------------------------------
plot_varimp(lm_imp, "LM Variable Importance")
plot_varimp(gam_imp, "GAM Variable Importance")
plot_varimp(rf_imp, "Random Forest Variable Importance")
plot_varimp(xgb_imp, "XGBoost Variable Importance")

# Combine the feature importance data into a single data frame
combine_varimp <- function(lm_imp, gam_imp, rf_imp, xgb_imp) {
  # Add model names to each importance data frame
  lm_imp$Model <- "Linear Regression"
  gam_imp$Model <- "Generalized Additive Model"
  rf_imp$Model <- "Random Forest"
  xgb_imp$Model <- "XGBoost"
  
  # Combine all model importance data frames
  combined_imp <- rbind(lm_imp, gam_imp, rf_imp, xgb_imp)
  
  # Make sure Features are factor to handle the plot properly
  combined_imp$Feature <- factor(combined_imp$Feature, levels = unique(combined_imp$Feature))
  
  return(combined_imp)
}

# Combine importance from all models
combined_imp <- combine_varimp(lm_imp, gam_imp, rf_imp, xgb_imp)

# Print the combined feature importance data
print(combined_imp)
```

```{r publishable feature importance plots}
###############################################
# Random Forest Feature Importance Figure
###############################################
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Extract RF importance from imputation 1
imp <- importance(rf_fit[[1]])

imp_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(imp),
  Importance = imp[, 1],
  row.names = NULL
)

# Clean variable labels

label_names <- c(
  ph_p_pds_001 = "PDS Parent: Growth Spurt",
  ph_p_pds__m_001 = "PDS Parent: Voice Change",
  ph_p_pds__m_002 = "PDS Parent: Facial Hair",
  ph_p_pds__f_001 = "PDS Parent: Breast Development",
  ph_p_pds__f_002 = "PDS Parent: Menarche Status",
  ph_y_pds_001 = "PDS Youth: Growth Spurt",
  ph_y_pds__m_001 = "PDS Youth: Voice Change",
  ph_y_pds__m_002 = "PDS Youth: Facial Hair",
  ph_y_pds__f_001 = "PDS Youth: Breast Development",
  ph_y_pds__f_002 = "PDS Youth: Menarche Status",
  ph_y_phs__ert_mean = "Testosterone",
  ph_p_pds_002 = "PDS Parent: Body Hair",
  ph_p_pds_003 = "PDS Parent: Skin Changes",
  ph_y_pds_002 = "PDS Youth: Body Hair",
  ph_y_pds_003 = "PDS Youth: Skin Changes",
  ph_y_phs__dhea_mean = "DHEA",
  ph_y_phs__hse_mean = "Estradiol"
)

imp_df <- imp_df %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(Variable, !!!label_names)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Importance) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = factor(Variable, levels = Variable)
  )

# Lollipop plot
ggplot(imp_df, aes(x = Importance, y = Variable)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = Importance, y = Variable, yend = Variable),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "gray70"
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    x = "% Feature Importance",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Male Adrenal Pubertal Age"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)
  )
```

```{r final full-sample pubertal age adrenal scoring}

# =========================================================
# FINAL FULL-SAMPLE SCORING MODEL FOR PUBERTAL AGE GAP
# =========================================================
# Purpose:
# This chunk is for constructing the final derived variable:
#   1) predicted pubertal age
#   2) predicted pubertal age z-score
#
# This is NOT for holdout performance evaluation.
# It is the final scoring stage after model strategy is chosen.
# =========================================================

library(dplyr)
library(mice)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(ggplot2)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Define outcome and predictor set
# ---------------------------------------------------------
target_str <- "ph_y_pds_age"

predictors_age_YESpuberty <- c(
  "ph_p_pds_002",#
  "ph_p_pds_003",#
  "ph_y_pds_002",#
  "ph_y_pds_003",#
  "ph_y_phs__dhea_mean"#
  )

formula_obj <- as.formula(
  paste(target_str, "~", paste(predictors_age_YESpuberty, collapse = " + "))
)

print(formula_obj)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Build final analytic sample
#    Keep only needed variables and require non-missing age
# ---------------------------------------------------------
final_analysis_df <- abcd_male %>%
  select(participant_id,all_of(c(target_str, predictors_age_YESpuberty))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(.data[[target_str]]))

final_ids <- final_analysis_df$participant_id
final_analysis_df_model <- final_analysis_df %>% select(-participant_id)

cat("Final analytic N =", nrow(final_analysis_df), "\n")

# Optional: inspect missingness
missing_counts <- sapply(final_analysis_df, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
print(missing_counts)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Multiple imputation on FULL analytic sample
# ---------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(999)

final_imp <- mice(
  final_analysis_df_model,
  m = 5,
  maxit = 10,
  seed = 999,
  printFlag = TRUE
)

final_sets <- complete(final_imp, action = "all")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Scale predictors
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Because this is the final scoring model on the full sample,
# scaling on the full imputed analytic sample is acceptable.
# To keep scaling consistent across imputations, estimate the
# centering/scaling parameters from imputation 1, then apply
# them to all imputed datasets.

numeric_predictors <- predictors_age_YESpuberty[
  sapply(final_sets[[1]][, predictors_age_YESpuberty, drop = FALSE], is.numeric)
]

pp_final <- preProcess(
  final_sets[[1]][, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE],
  method = c("center", "scale")
)

final_sets <- lapply(final_sets, function(df) {
  df[, numeric_predictors] <- predict(pp_final, df[, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE])
  df
})

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Fit final Random Forest model on each imputed dataset
# ---------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(999)

final_rf <- lapply(seq_along(final_sets), function(i) {
  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("Fitting FINAL Random Forest â€” Imputation", i, "\n")
  cat("==============================\n")

  randomForest(
    formula = formula_obj,
    data = final_sets[[i]],
    ntree = 500,
    importance = TRUE,
    do.trace = 50
  )
})

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Generate predicted pubertal age for each imputation
# ---------------------------------------------------------
pred_mat <- sapply(seq_along(final_rf), function(i) {
  predict(final_rf[[i]], newdata = final_sets[[i]])
})

# Average predicted pubertal age across imputations
pubertal_age_pred_mean <- rowMeans(pred_mat)

# Also compute optional between-imputation SD of predictions
pubertal_age_pred_sd <- apply(pred_mat, 1, sd)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Create final scored dataset
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Use imputation 1 as the base dataset for storing pooled
# prediction summaries. The prediction itself is averaged
# across all imputations.

full_df_scored <- final_sets[[1]]
full_df_scored$participant_id <- final_ids
full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_age <- pubertal_age_pred_mean
full_df_scored$pubertal_age_pred_sd <- pubertal_age_pred_sd

full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_timing_z <- as.numeric(
  scale(full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_age))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Quick distribution checks
# ---------------------------------------------------------
cat("\nPredicted pubertal age summary:\n")
print(summary(full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_age))

cat("\nPubertal age pred sd:\n")
print(summary(full_df_scored$pubertal_age_pred_sd))


# Q-Q plot
qqnorm(full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_age,
       main = "Q-Q Plot of Adrenal Pubertal Age Z-Score")

mu <- mean(full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_age, na.rm = TRUE)
sigma <- sd(full_df_scored$adrenal_pubertal_age, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(full_df_scored, aes(x = adrenal_pubertal_age)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30,
                 alpha=0.8) +

  # Mean
  geom_vline(xintercept = mu, linewidth = 1) +

  # Â±1 SD
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = c(mu - sigma, mu + sigma),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  # Â±2 SD
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = c(mu - 2*sigma, mu + 2*sigma),
    linetype = "dotted",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Male Adrenal Pubertal Age",
    x = "Adrenal Pubertal Age",
    y = "Count"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()



# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Optional: examine variable importance
# ---------------------------------------------------------
rf_importance_list <- lapply(final_rf, importance)

# Example: importance from first imputation
print(rf_importance_list[[1]])
####
```
#```{r Save Pubertal Age Z}
full_df_scored$chronological_age_wave0 <- full_df_scored[[target_str]]

pubertal_export <- full_df_scored[, c(
  "participant_id",
  "chronological_age_wave0",
  "adrenal_pubertal_timing_z"
)]

saveRDS(pubertal_export, "adrenal_pubertal_timing_male_wave0.rds")
#```


Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
